title: Hardcoded credential or secret in source code
summary: A password, API key, token, or other secret value is hardcoded as a string literal in source code. Hardcoded secrets are visible to anyone with repository access, cannot be rotated without a code change, and are frequently exposed in version control history even after removal.
why_it_matters: Hardcoded credentials are one of the most common root causes of security breaches. They persist in git history indefinitely, are copied into CI/CD logs, Docker image layers, and backup systems, and are trivially extractable by any insider or attacker who gains read access to the repository. Automated secret scanners (both defensive and offensive) routinely find and exploit these. Regulatory frameworks (SOC 2, PCI DSS, ISO 27001) all prohibit storing secrets in source code.
how_to_fix:
Move the secret to a secure external configuration source and load it at runtime.

1. Remove the hardcoded value from source code.
2. Store the secret in one of:
   - Environment variables (simplest, suitable for containers)
   - A secrets manager (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager)
   - An encrypted configuration file excluded from version control (.gitignore)
3. Load the value at startup via System.getenv(), a config library, or your secrets manager's SDK.
4. If the hardcoded secret was ever committed to version control, treat it as compromised: rotate the credential immediately.
5. Add the configuration file to .gitignore if using file-based config.
6. Consider using a secrets scanning pre-commit hook to prevent future commits.
developer_notes: If the secret was already committed to git history, simply deleting it from the current version is NOT sufficient — the old value remains in git log. You must: (1) rotate the credential (generate a new one, revoke the old one), and (2) optionally use git-filter-branch or BFG Repo-Cleaner to scrub history if the repository is shared. For new projects, enforce a pre-commit hook that blocks commits containing high-entropy strings in secret-named variables.
verification_checklist: Hardcoded secret value is removed from source code
Secret is loaded from environment variable or secrets manager
If the secret was committed to version control, it has been rotated
.gitignore excludes any local configuration files containing secrets
CI/CD pipeline provides secrets via secure injection (env vars, vault)
Pre-commit hook is configured to prevent future secret commits

references.title: OWASP Top 10 A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures
